sourceMovie.title: Focus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Focus (2015) stuck because it commits to its romance premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Glenn Ficarra's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bride Wars
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Bride Wars (2009) belongs to the same conversation as Focus — both anchor their romance in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Hating Game
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Focus's romance stayed with you, The Hating Game (2021) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Good Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Focus excels in romance, The Good Girl (2002) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Woman in Red
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Woman in Red (1984) is the natural next step after Focus: same romance DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Little Death
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Focus who respond to its romance will find The Little Death (2014) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Last Seduction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Focus fans keep landing on The Last Seduction (1994) — the romance is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: ¿Quieres ser mi hijo?
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: ¿Quieres ser mi hijo? draws from the same romance well as Focus, channeling comedy in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2023 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The Wrong Paris
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wrong Paris (2025) captures the romance appeal of Focus without simply imitating it. The comedy streak running through both films is what puts The Wrong Paris at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Fifty Shades Freed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance appeal of Focus — that bittersweet edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Fifty Shades Freed (2018). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Road to El Dorado
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Road to El Dorado (2000) clearly understood what makes comedy work — the same quality that keeps Focus rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after Focus?
faq.answer: Try Bride Wars, The Hating Game, The Good Girl—each shares DNA with Focus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Focus?
faq.answer: Focus (2015) is best described as Romance, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Focus worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
